meta-description: Learn about the affordability exemption by reviewing the definition in the HealthCare.gov Glossary.
title: Affordability exemption
excerpt: An exemption that’s needed when applying for Catastrophic coverage for people 30 years or older whose coverage is unaffordable. Affordability exemptions are one type of exemption that someone can claim to qualify for Catastrophic Coverage, along with hardship exemptions.
content:
An exemption that’s needed when applying for Catastrophic coverage for people 30 years or older whose coverage is unaffordable. Affordability exemptions are one type of exemption that someone can claim to qualify for Catastrophic Coverage, along with hardship exemptions.

You can qualify for an affordability exemption if the lowest-priced coverage available to you, through either a Marketplace or job-based plan, would cost more than 7.97% of your household income.
